title: Jiaju’s E-Portfolio
text: Currently studying at UBC as a master student, I have the privilege to live and learn as an uninvited guest on the traditional, ancestral and unceded territories of the Musqueam people. I worked with a First Nation forestry venture, and I deeply appreciate this opportunity to build a real connection with “the people of the land” - the Lil’wat First Nation. I am working on MGEM capstone project on species detection of Garry oak trees in Victoria’s urban forest using remote sensing.

title: Resume
text: I leverage the power of remote sensing, geospatial analysis, and forestry in my work. My strengths include turning spatial data into maps and reports for decision-making and further analysis.

title: Skills & Deliverables
text:
Selected skills, tools, and deliverable from my MGEM studies.

## Overview

Through the MGEM program, I developed skills in:

- remote sensing and raster processing
- GIS mapping and cartography
- LiDAR and aerial image analysis
- spatial analysis in R
- basic coding and data visualization

—

title: Jiaju’s E-Portfolio
text:
Garry oak detection map representation

Description: Expected Garry Oak crown classification maps: (a) Fusion model (LiDAR + 4 band orthophoto), (b) LiDAR-only. Deep green polygons show predicted Garry Oak, light green polygons show predicted non-Garry Oak, and red points are the 2024 Garry Oak inventory.

title: Resume
Capstone Project
text:
This project mapped Garry oak trees in Victoria using remote sensing data.
I used LiDAR, orthophotos, and Random Forest models to detect Garry oak crowns.

title: Capstone Project
text:
This project mapped Garry oak trees in Victoria using remote sensing data.
I used LiDAR, orthophotos, and Random Forest models to detect Garry oak crowns.

title: Capstone Project
text:
Why It Matters
Garry oak is an important native species.
This project shows how remote sensing can help map it more efficiently.

title: Capstone Project
text:
Data and Methods
I used LiDAR data, 4-band orthophotos, and ground truth reference data.
I built two models: 1. - LiDAR-only 2. - Fusion (LiDAR + orthophoto)

title: Capstone Project
text:
Workflow designs
Data processing → feature extraction → modelling → validation → mapping

title: Capstone Project
text:
Model Performance
The LiDAR-only model worked reasonably well.
The fusion model performed better overall.

title: Capstone Project
text:
Key Result
Adding orthophoto information improved Garry oak detection.
The fusion model gave more accurate results than the LiDAR-only model.

title: Capstone Project
text:
Garry oak detection map representation

Description: Expected Garry Oak crown classification maps: (a) Fusion model (LiDAR + 4 band orthophoto), (b) LiDAR-only. Deep green polygons show predicted Garry Oak, light green polygons show predicted non-Garry Oak, and red points are the 2024 Garry Oak inventory.

title: Skills & Deliverables
text:
GEM 520 – Remote Sensing I
Focus: raster processing, satellite imagery, vegetation indices
Main tools: terra, ggplot2

Skills

raster handling
band math
NDVI calculation
simple classification
plotting raster outputs



Example R code: NDVI with simple raster layers

library(terra)

Warning: package 'terra' was built under R version 4.5.1


terra 1.8.74

red <- rast(nrows = 4, ncols = 4, vals = c(
  0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.2,
  0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.2,
  0.2, 0.4, 0.5, 0.3,
  0.1, 0.2, 0.2, 0.1
))

nir <- rast(nrows = 4, ncols = 4, vals = c(
  0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.6,
  0.5, 0.6, 0.7, 0.5,
  0.6, 0.8, 0.9, 0.7,
  0.4, 0.5, 0.6, 0.4
))

ndvi <- (nir - red) / (nir + red)
plot(ndvi, main = "Example NDVI")

title: Skills & Deliverables
text:
GEM 521 – Remote Sensing II
Focus: ALS, hyperspectral thinking, feature extraction, classification
Main tools: terra, ggplot2, dplyr

Skills

working with spectral and structural variables
comparing classes
extracting meaningful features
classification thinking



Example R code: comparing spectral signatures

library(ggplot2)

Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.1

spectra <- data.frame(
  wavelength = c(450, 550, 650, 850, 1600, 2200),
  vegetation = c(0.08, 0.12, 0.05, 0.52, 0.30, 0.18),
  soil = c(0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30, 0.35, 0.32)
)

spectra_long <- reshape(
  spectra,
  varying = c("vegetation", "soil"),
  v.names = "reflectance",
  timevar = "class",
  times = c("Vegetation", "Soil"),
  direction = "long"
)

ggplot(spectra_long, aes(x = wavelength, y = reflectance, color = class)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Example Spectral Profiles",
       x = "Wavelength (nm)",
       y = "Reflectance")

title: Skills & Deliverables
text:
FCOR 511 – Cartography and Visualization
Focus: map design, layout, communication, visual clarity
Main tools: ggplot2, sf

Skills

map readability
symbol choices
clear visual communication
making figures more professional



Example R code: simple thematic map style

library(ggplot2)

areas <- data.frame(
  neighborhood = c("North", "South", "East", "West"),
  park_cover = c(12, 28, 19, 35)
)

ggplot(areas, aes(x = neighborhood, y = park_cover)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Example Thematic Figure",
       x = "Area",
       y = "Park Cover (%)")

integrating social and environmental ideas
thinking beyond technical analysis
considering multiple values in decision-making

title: Skills & Deliverables
text:
FCOR 599 Capstone Project

**Focus:** Garry oak detection using LiDAR and orthophotos
**Main tools:** `terra`, `lidR`, `sf`, `dplyr`, `ggplot2`, `randomForest`

### Skills
- crown-level feature extraction
- classification workflow design
- model comparison
- map production
- interpreting accuracy results

### Example R code: simple crown classification


library(dplyr)

Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.1



Attaching package: 'dplyr'


The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag


The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

crowns <- data.frame(  
  crown_id = 1:8,  
  height_m = c(12, 18, 9, 16, 7, 20, 11, 15),  
  ndvi = c(0.72, 0.81, 0.55, 0.78, 0.49, 0.84, 0.61, 0.76))

crowns <- crowns %>%  
  mutate(prediction = ifelse(height_m > 10 & ndvi > 0.70, "Garry oak", "Other"))

crowns

  crown_id height_m ndvi prediction
1        1       12 0.72  Garry oak
2        2       18 0.81  Garry oak
3        3        9 0.55      Other
4        4       16 0.78  Garry oak
5        5        7 0.49      Other
6        6       20 0.84  Garry oak
7        7       11 0.61      Other
8        8       15 0.76  Garry oak
